data.body:
“See you tomorrow,” Galen called out. 
The Ambassador smiled. “It’s been a long day,” she said. She started towards the walk that led to her quarters.
“I’ll walk with you. It’s late,” Galen said.
“Thank you. I’d like that,” the Ambassador said. 

2/14
data.author: did:plc:fkvdf5xwcvc6wes4wdunucmc

data.body:
“When I went into shock at the library, it was your strength and the hope in your eyes that brought me back,” Galen said. 
“Sentar did quite a bit, too,” the Ambassador said. 
“I followed you,” Galen said. He turned to look at her, a tear in his eye. He pulled her close. 

7/14
data.author: did:plc:fkvdf5xwcvc6wes4wdunucmc

data.body:
“I know. But I will be right here tomorrow,” Galen said gently. He pointed at her door step and smiled. 
“Coffee at six on the patio?” the Ambassador asked.
“Maybe seven? It’s later than you think,” Galen said. He looked up at the stars. 

12/14
